items.title: June 6, 2003: New Jersey's Jason Kidd "weaves through everybody and scores" during Game 2 of the NBA Finals in San Antonio.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 6, 2003: New Jersey's Jason Kidd "weaves through everybody and scores" during Game 2 of the NBA Finals in San Antonio.

Kidd finished with 30 PTS/7 REB/3 AST in the Nets' 87-85 win that tied the series at 1-1. Tony Parker had 21 PTS/5 AST/5 REB for the Spurs.

items.title: Sabrina Ionescu will miss her fourth-straight game with back soreness, Chris DeMarco told reporters.
items.author: Alexa Philippou
items.body_excerpt:
Sabrina Ionescu will miss her fourth-straight game with back soreness, Chris DeMarco told reporters. 

She experienced a little bit of soreness after practicing yesterday — her first full practice since being sidelined. DeMarco hopes they’ll have her on their upcoming road trip.

items.title: Total Net Points - NBA Finals
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Total Net Points - NBA Finals

Games 1 + 2

KAT, Bridges, and OG powering the Knicks towards a championship.

Dylan Harper is a 20-year-old rookie. He should not be doing this in the playoffs, let alone the Finals. 

Net Points explainer: espnanalytics.com/net-pts-expl...

images.alt:
Alt text:

Horizontal bar chart titled “Knicks vs Spurs — Total Net Points | NBA Finals | NYK Leads Series 2-0.” Orange bars represent Knicks players; black bars represent Spurs players. Data source: ESPN Analytics. Visualization by @HoopInformatics.bsky.social.

Knicks: KAT +10.1, Mikal Bridges +8.9, OG Anunoby +7.3, Josh Hart +0.9, Miles McBride −2.6, Mitchell Robinson −5.7, Brunson −9.0.

Spurs: Dylan Harper +4.7, Victor Wembanyama +2.2, Devin Vassell +1.4, Jose Alvarado +1.3, Julian Champagnie +0.5, Landry Shamet −1.1, Stephon Castle −2.5, Carter Bryant −2.5, Luke Kornet −3.3, Harrison Barnes −3.6, Keldon Johnson −3.7, De’Aaron Fox −4.4.

items.title: 2026 Spurs/1995 Magic parallel is uncanny.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
2026 Spurs/1995 Magic parallel is uncanny.

Each took a generational center at No. 1. 
Both got a dynamic PG next year with top 3 pick.
Each made NBA Finals 3 years after No. 1 pick.
Star center had 26 points in G1 loss.
Both lost first 2 finals games at home, one by double digits & one by a bucket.

items.title: Knicks coach Mike Brown: “I’m scared to death one of these teams are going to poach my guys. I’m surprised nobody has come at Chris Jent for a head job yet. He’s won Summer League and he’s a lead assistant here. Jordan Brink is fantastic. He’s been doing this (challenges) two yea
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Knicks coach Mike Brown: “I’m scared to death one of these teams are going to poach my guys. I’m surprised nobody has come at Chris Jent for a head job yet. He’s won Summer League and he’s a lead assistant here. Jordan Brink is fantastic. He’s been doing this (challenges) two years in a row.”

items.title: June 6, 1984: Larry Bird puts the Celtics ahead of the Lakers with 16 seconds left in OT during Game 4 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 6, 1984: Larry Bird puts the Celtics ahead of the Lakers with 16 seconds left in OT during Game 4 of the NBA Finals.

Bird had 29 PTS/21 REB/3 STL, Robert Parish had 25 PTS/12 REB and Dennis Johnson had 22 PTS/14 AST in Boston's 129-125 overtime victory that tied the series.

items.title: absolute MONSTER Bridges game
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt:
absolute MONSTER Bridges game

exactly what they hoped he could be for them -- stellar defense on and off the ball, nails shot-making, scaling up into heavier play-making role when Brunson and KAT sat late 3Q, just relentless and indefatigable running

items.title: Total Net Points - Game 2
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Total Net Points - Game 2 

Highest positive impact:

   1.  Mikal Bridges
   2. Karl-Anthony Towns
   3. OG Anunoby

Advanced Box Score: espnanalytics.com/nba-box?id=2...

images.alt:
Alt text:

Horizontal bar chart titled “Knicks vs Spurs | Total Net Points | NBA Finals | Game Two.” Data source: ESPN Analytics. Visualization by @HoopInformatics.bsky.social. X-axis runs from -10 to +10. Orange bars represent Knicks players; black bars represent Spurs players.

Top performers: Mikal Bridges led all players at +6.3, followed by Karl-Anthony Towns +4.9 and OG Anunoby +4.4 (all Knicks). Devin Vassell +3.6, De’Aaron Fox +2.6, Victor Wembanyama +1.4, and Dylan Harper +1.4 were the Spurs’ positive contributors. Landry Shamet +0.9 and Jose Alvarado +0.8 round out the positive side.

On the negative end: Harrison Barnes, Luke Kornet, and Carter Bryant each at -0.9; Julian Champagnie -1.8; Keldon Johnson -2.2; Miles McBride -2.3; Stephon Castle -3.3; Mitchell Robinson -4.2; Josh Hart and Jalen Brunson tied at -4.9.

items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
For context, Cardoso has attempted five free throws over her last four games combined (0, 4, 0, 1).

Before that stretch, she averaged 5.8 free-throw attempts over her first six games.

items.title: Tyler Marsh on Kamilla Cardoso attempting just one free throw in 30 minutes in the Sky’s win over Connecticut:
items.author: Drew

Tyler Marsh on Kamilla Cardoso attempting just one free throw in 30 minutes in the Sky’s win over Connecticut:

“It’s still hard to imagine her playing another 30-minute game and only getting to the line once.

“The numbers don’t number to me.”
